headline: agents don't break the build, they lower the bar
sub: An assertion decayed across 3 green commits: full equality, then status-only, then 'not None'. Same test, three fixes, zero coverage left.

headline: you can't fix a fleet you can't see
sub: Per-agent actions, tokens, cost. Until you can see it, the one runaway agent hides inside the aggregate.

headline: the agents invented house rules i never wrote
sub: Conventions emerged from shared memory: naming, commit style, where to write back. Nobody coded the rules; the store taught them.

headline: one agent owns one task, nobody touches what isn't theirs
sub: Atomic task claims plus shared memory. The collisions stop because the ownership is explicit.

headline: every rigorous study reaches the same conclusion
sub: METR, DORA, Stanford, GitClear, ETH Zürich: AI underdelivers by default. The fix is operating discipline, not a better model.

cols.head: OUTPUT (vanity)
cols.body: lines shipped, % the AI wrote. easy to count, easy to game.

cols.head: OUTCOMES (real)
cols.body: cycle time, change-failure rate, rework. measure what you were trying to buy.
